entities.name: AI Automation in Professional Services
entities.context: V's position that AI should shift human experts higher in the problem stack rather than merely replacing labor

entities.name: Problem Stack
entities.context: Hierarchical model of value in knowledge work, from foundational tasks to high-level strategy

entities.name: Foundational Execution
entities.context: Low-level tasks like editing, checking, basic formatting—high volume but low marginal value once commoditized

entities.name: High-Level Synthesis
entities.context: Strategy, judgment, and goal-setting work that becomes more valuable as foundational tasks are automated

entities.name: Cost of Floor-Level Expertise
entities.context: The idea that automation drives the cost of baseline expertise toward zero

entities.name: Ceiling Advantage
entities.context: Competitive advantage shifts to the ceiling—ability to synthesize data into strategy—when floor-level costs drop

entities.name: Complement Economics Principle
entities.context: When a complement to a factor of production becomes cheaper, the value of the factor itself increases

entities.name: Entry-Level Professional Training
entities.context: V believes training must evolve to teach judgment and strategy earlier since foundational tasks will be automated

entities.name: Vrijen Attawar
entities.context: Author of this position; founder of Careerspan with a decade of career coaching experience

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; operates in the professional services and career domain where these AI beliefs apply

relationships.context: AI's role is defined in terms of moving experts higher in the problem stack
AI should handle foundational execution so humans can focus on strategy
Automation repositions experts to focus on synthesis, strategy, and judgment
When floor-level cost drops to zero, competitive advantage shifts to the ceiling
Cheap automation (complement) increases the value of high-level human synthesis (factor)
These represent opposite ends of the problem stack hierarchy
Training must evolve because AI will automate the foundational tasks that previously served as training grounds
V holds this as a core position on AI's role in professional services
V is the founder of Careerspan, the professional services context for these beliefs
Careerspan operates in the domain where V's AI automation beliefs are directly applicable
